classification.domain: security and privacy transparency in generative AI

topic.what: User needs for security and privacy transparency in consumer-facing generative AI tools
topic.how: Semi-structured interviews and design sessions with 21 U.S. GenAI users
topic.why_matters: Current S&P communication fails to drive adoption decisions; users lack actionable information for high-stakes decisions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Perceived adequacy of S&P information available to users
characteristics.evidence: Users perceived existing S&P info as incomplete
characteristics.context: Users reported gaps in understanding data practices

characteristics.what_it_measures: Trustworthiness of S&P sources provided by GenAI companies
characteristics.evidence: Users lacked confidence in company-provided S&P disclosures
characteristics.context: Users sought independent evaluations as credibility signals

characteristics.what_itasures: Whether S&P information drives initial GenAI adoption
characteristics.evidence: S&P info rarely drove adoption; users relied on proxies like popularity
characteristics.context: 21 participants across interviews and design sessions

characteristics.what_it_measures: User willingness to continue using GenAI based on S&P transparency
characteristics.evidence: Uncertainty about S&P practices constrained willingness to use, especially for high-stakes tasks
characteristics.context: Users expressed hesitancy for health/legal/financial applications

characteristics.what_it_measures: Perceived utility of S&P disclosures for decision-making
characteristics.evidence: Users found existing S&P info ineffective for informed decision-making
characteristics.context: Users called for trustworthy independent evaluations and usable interfaces

good_for: Privacy label design for AI systems
requires: User study participants with GenAI experience

novelty: Empirical evidence that S&P info rarely drives GenAI adoption
Synthesis of five transparency design dimensions
User-preferred practices including independent evaluations and on-demand disclosure
Recommendations for researchers, designers, and policymakers
